title: Replays / Records
description: Capture a live you-agent-factory run as a replay artifact, find the saved recording after shutdown, or re-run from saved history without dispatching live workers again.
openingSummary: Replays / Records is the you-agent-factory reference for capturing live runs as replay artifacts and replaying saved history without dispatching live workers again.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Replays / Records is the web reference for you-agent-factory record and replay behavior. Use it when you need to capture a live factory run as a replay-compatible artifact, find the saved recording after shutdown, or re-run from saved history without dispatching live workers again.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A recording is a replay-compatible artifact that stores observed runtime history from a live factory run. Record mode writes that history during a live run. Replay mode reads an existing artifact and reuses the saved history instead of starting live workers again. This page is the record and replay documentation surface; it is not factory-session pause, resume, or status inspection.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Start from a normal live you run when you want a recording written by default. Use this page to learn how record and replay modes differ, where default artifacts land, which run-control flags change recording behavior, and how to replay a saved path later. Deeper flag tables, copyable examples, and retention guidance expand in the sections that follow as this reference is completed.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web record and replay reference for you-agent-factory. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not factory-session pause or resume, not mock-worker selection contracts, and not a logs, metrics, or API reference.
